description: Taxonomy of Fraud from the Stanford Center on Longevity (2015), including hierarchy nodes and attribute tags.
name: Taxonomy of Fraud

values.description: Family member perpetrator (source PDF reuses FP code)
Expected products, services, and other items

values.value: Weight-loss products and health supplement scams
Other fake credit lines and loans
Unnecessary/overpriced repairs, or repairs never performed
Unauthorized billing for products or services

values.description: Vending machines or ATM leasing scam
Expected winnings in prize, lottery, grant, or other windfall
Expected benefit is avoiding fake debt consequences

values.value: False identity as natural disaster or national tragedy survivor

values.description: Expected outcome is fostering a relationship
Other friends or relatives imposter scam

values.value: Fraud against government agencies, programs, regulations, and society

values.description: Examples include welfare, disability, medicare, medicaid fraud
Examples include immigration, voting, tax, stamp fraud
Examples include insider trading and environmental fraud

values.value: Fraud against non-governmental businesses or organizations

values.description: Examples include corruption, asset misappropriation, financial statement fraud
values.value: Occupational Fraud, committed by internal perpetrator

values.description: Examples include insurance fraud, bank fraud, fraudulent suppliers
